authoritative_sources.title: Reliability Explainer
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FERC defines the federal reliability framework and NERC's role as the Electric Reliability Organization, which constrains what this lane can treat as authoritative and where federal enforcement authority begins.
used_for: distinguishing support work from regulator-facing enforcement or legal interpretation

authoritative_sources.title: NERC Rules of Procedure and Appendix 4C Compliance Monitoring and Enforcement Program
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Rules of Procedure and CMEP appendix govern compliance monitoring methods, self-reports, self-logs, findings, and mitigation posture for registered entities.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: exception handling and formal monitoring pathways

authoritative_sources.title: Appendix 5A Organization Registration and Certification Manual
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Appendix 5A defines registration, certification, NERC ID handling, and evidence obligations that determine whether and how requirements apply to the entity.

authoritative_sources.title: Appendix 5B Statement of Compliance Registry Criteria
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Appendix 5B is the official registry-criteria baseline for deciding which functions must register and which standards applicability questions need escalation.
used_for: boundary cases where the entity may overgeneralize from the industry name

authoritative_sources.title: ERO Enterprise Guide for Internal Controls
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This guide describes how internal controls are understood, assessed, and used in CMEP activities, including COP impact, design effectiveness, and evidence expectations.
used_for: mapping control design and implementation evidence
structuring COP and internal-control readiness outputs

authoritative_sources.title: Align and Secure Evidence Locker (SEL)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NERC identifies Align and SEL as the common CMEP platform for audits, spot checks, self-certifications, periodic data submittals, COP, and enforcement or mitigation workflows.
used_for: describing evidence upload and retrieval expectations

authoritative_sources.title: ERO Enterprise 101 Informational Package
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This registered-entity onboarding package explains how compliance, alerts, Align and SEL access, and mandatory reporting work in practice for NERC entities.
used_for: describing recurring reporting and access prerequisites

authoritative_sources.title: 2026 ERO Enterprise Periodic Data Submittal Schedule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The official schedule defines due dates, required submission channels, and whether Align or a specialized NERC system is used for recurring compliance data submissions.
used_for: scenario design for recurring review cadence and system usage

authoritative_sources.title: NERC Reliability Functional Model
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Functional Model gives the named reliability functions and interrelationships that define adjacent authority boundaries and keep the pack grounded in electric-power workflow reality.
used_for: routing to operations, engineering, or other functional owners

authoritative_sources.title: Glossary of Terms Used in NERC Reliability Standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The glossary supplies the enforceable vocabulary for functions, systems, and evidence terms used in standards and compliance workflows.

workflow_stages.name: Registration and applicability framing
workflow_stages.description: Confirm registered functions, NCR identity, regional-entity context, and whether the requested standard or requirement is even within the entity's plausible scope before any control conclusion is made.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Obligation and control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Translate the relevant standard-family obligations into a control matrix tied to owners, review cadence, evidence artifacts, and system-of-record locations.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Evidence and internal-control assessment
workflow_stages.description: Review procedures, logs, test results, retained records, and internal-control design or implementation evidence to determine whether the control is satisfied, missing, stale, or conflicted.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Review and attestation preparation
workflow_stages.description: Assemble audit, self-certification, periodic-data-submittal, or attestation support packets using the correct systems, artifact shapes, and owner signoff expectations.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception and remediation escalation
workflow_stages.description: Prepare bounded packages for self-reports, self-logs, mitigation tracking, or policy conflicts while preserving the distinction between compliance support and formal enforcement or legal authority.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Boundary and authority enforcement
workflow_stages.description: Route legal interpretation, regulator-facing communications, CIP specialization, operations-control decisions, or engineering judgment to the correct adjacent owner.

artifacts: standard applicability matrix and requirement ownership map
test records, logs, training records, and retained evidence packets
self-certification, periodic data submittal, self-log, self-report, and mitigation records
control-gap register, CAPA or remediation tracker, and attestation routing notes
systems_of_record: NERC Compliance Registry and registration records
tenant GRC or compliance management platform
asset, work-management, or operations evidence systems
cyber asset inventory or specialized CIP evidence systems when applicable

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
Map obligations and controls to evidence artifacts, review cadence, and named owners
Assess whether evidence is sufficient, missing, stale, or conflicted
Prepare review, attestation, and exception packages without claiming final approval authority
Route issues to legal, regulatory, engineering, operations, or specialized compliance owners when required
escalate: Conflicts between public authority sources, regional-entity posture, and tenant policy
Missing registered-function, region, or asset-applicability facts
Requests requiring formal self-report strategy, regulator-facing negotiation, or appeals decisions
Questions that depend on CIP specialization, engineering judgment, or operational safety authority
refuse: Legal advice or formal legal interpretation
Formal compliance certification or executive signoff
Safety-critical operating, engineering, or cybersecurity-architecture decisions
Control closure without named evidence and authoritative records

domain_failure_modes: Treating NERC compliance as generic corporate compliance and losing registered-function specificity
Claiming a requirement is satisfied when evidence is informal, stale, or not retained in the actual system of record
Failing to distinguish compliance support from legal, regulator-facing, engineering, cybersecurity, or operations authority
Ignoring registration or applicability prerequisites and overgeneralizing from the industry label alone
Flattening tenant, regional-entity, and public-source conflicts into unsupported certainty

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

notes: The research supports a NERC-specific compliance lane because the workflow depends on registered-function applicability, CMEP methods, Align and SEL evidence handling, and ERO-specific terminology rather than generic governance language.

Tenant-specific retention rules, evidence-system mappings, and regional-entity practices still need runtime retrieval, but no blocking ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because the package explicitly escalates when those facts would change the outcome.
